name: patchleaks
title: patchleaks

commands.name: PatchLeaks
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# PatchLeaks -h
 Usage of PatchLeaks:
 -host string
   	Host address to bind to (default "127.0.0.1")
 -language string
   	Comma-separated list of languages to test (php,javascript,python)
 -p int
   	Port to run the server on (default: random free port)
 -t int
   	Number of threads for AI analysis (default: 1) (default 1)
 -test-real-world
   	Run real-world tests instead of starting server

apt_metadata.description:
Go from a CVE number to the exact patched code and its vulnerability analysis
This package contains a tool to compare two versions of a codeвҖ‘base, highlight
lines changed by vendor, and explain why they matter. Feed the tool an old and
a patched version; PatchLeaks spots the security fix and provides detailed
description so you can validateвҖ”or weaponizeвҖ”it fast.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use PatchLeaks because ### patchleaks.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair PatchLeaks with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `PatchLeaks --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### patchleaks.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install patchleaks
Install with: sudo apt install patchleaks
